text:
Once upon a time, in a quaint little town named Whiskerburgh, there lived a dog named Sparkle. She was energetic, playful, and had the finest mop of curls you'd ever see on a golden retriever. Sparkle worked as a waitress at the local café, her favorite escape from the drudgery of petting zoos and old barks.

Every day, she would greet her friends with a warm hug and a playful wag. But something was always amiss—she seemed to carry a shadow. Late one autumn afternoon, while serving a customer a delicious bowl of pumpkin pie, Sparkle felt a heavy weight settle into her bones.

She dropped the tray.

The customer gasped. Sparkle laid there motionless, tilting her head to one side.

When the paramedics arrived, they discovered something unexpected—her heart had stopped. The local vets said she had contracted a rare illness unknown during their lifetimes.
